reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures the informal statement. The hypothesis `Odd p ∧ p.Prime` exactly encodes "p is an odd prime" (excluding 2). The sum `∑ i ∈ Finset.range (p-1), (i+1) * n^i` expands to 1 + 2n + 3n² + ... + (p-1)n^(p-2), matching F(n). The conclusion `¬(F a ≡ F b [MOD p])` expresses "F(a) − F(b) is not divisible by p". The only notable encoding choice is using `Finset.Icc 1 p` = {1,...,p} instead of {0,...,p-1} as in the informal, but both are complete residue systems mod p, and since F(p) ≡ F(0) (mod p) (all terms with n^i for i≥1 vanish mod p), and the conclusion only involves values mod p, the two quantifications give logically equivalent statements. This is a standard faithful encoding.
